response.justification: Strong foundational engineering credentials with 20 years of experience and demonstrated staff-level technical leadership. However, critical gaps in required AI/GenAI expertise undermine fit for this role. Only ~2 months of AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP) at current role, no Python experience listed (required), and no evidence of RAG, vector databases, or multi-agent system implementations. Could transition into the specialization given strong fundamentals and recent interest, but lacks the 8+ years of production AI experience the role demands.
strengths: Deep staff-level software engineering track record (20 years) with proven ability to lead technical discovery, mentor teams, and drive engineering excellence across diverse domains
Direct exposure to MCP and agentic workflow frameworks in current role at re:cinq, demonstrating active engagement with the GenAI stack and relevant tech
Strong TypeScript and AWS cloud infrastructure expertise, with experience scaling systems to handle high-volume production workloads (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby)
concerns: Python not mentioned anywhere on resume despite being a required core technology for this role; all recent TypeScript-heavy positions may indicate language preference mismatch
Only 1-2 months of proven AI/GenAI production experience (current MCP work) versus 8+ years required; background is general software engineering, not AI-driven solutions
No demonstrated experience with vector databases, retrieval-augmented generation architectures, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI); core technical gaps for the role

key_factors.factor: Recent MCP and agentic framework engagement
